_meta.purpose: Custom per-tier alternates for ruflo, pointing every tier at the LiteLLM gateway. Activate with: export CLAUDE_FLOW_ROUTER_OPENROUTER_ALTS=$PWD/ruflo-tiers.json  (plus the env block in docs/guide/reference/tiers-and-routing.md → Integrating your tools → Ruflo / Claude-Flow). Ruflo keeps doing complexity scoring + bandit learning; the gateway decides what physically serves each tier.
_meta.note: Costs below are what the ROUTER should believe (local ≈ 0), so its cost-aware paths prefer local. Re-benchmark with your own hardware per docs/guide/reference/observability.md (Quality-regression harness).
_meta.locality_note: schema_version 2 is FORWARD-COMPATIBLE and non-breaking: the `tiers` map shape ruflo/LiteLLM consume is unchanged (they ignore `_meta` and any extra per-tier keys), and every tier keeps its v1 defaults. The only addition is a per-tier `locality` field declaring each tier's DEFAULT physical placement (local vs cloud). Per-REQUEST locality is a RUNTIME concern, not a schema flip: the router's `pinnedPrivate` lane (scripts/lib/router.mjs) resolves such requests to tier-private — a local-only gateway alias with no fallback chain — which is deliberately NOT in this alts map so nothing here can weaken it. A promoted per-question router would materialize the full v2 shape; this file only declares the capability.

haiku.rationale: Local Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (MoE, ~3B active, SWE-bench 73.4%) via gateway alias tier-fast; effectively $0 marginal cost. See docs/guide/reference/hardware-and-models.md.

sonnet.rationale: Local dense Qwen3.6-27B (SWE-bench Verified 77.2%) via gateway alias tier-heavy. See docs/guide/reference/hardware-and-models.md.

opus.rationale: Frontier alias — gateway fails over Claude→GPT→Gemini and enforces daily budgets.
